tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and imagine Jesus sitting next to a poor person, showing them equal respect and dignity. Breathe deeply and ask God to remove any hidden biases in your heart.

two_step.reflect_question: How might you be unconsciously showing favoritism in your workplace, church, or social circles?

options.text: By always listening more to people with status or authority
options.response: Intentionally seek out and value perspectives from those with less social power

options.text: By giving preferential treatment based on appearance or wealth
options.response: Practice treating every person with equal respect and genuine interest

options.text: By avoiding conversations with people different from me
options.response: Deliberately engage with diverse individuals, listening to their stories

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a scene where two people from very different backgrounds are seated equally at the same table, showing they are valued the same

reevaluation.question: How has reading James 2 challenged your understanding of genuine faith?
options: I now see faith is more about actions than just beliefs
I recognize my tendency to judge people superficially
I want to practically demonstrate love to everyone
I see the importance of treating all people with equal dignity

three_choice.question: Which aspect of spiritual judgment challenges you most?

choices.expansion: Recognizing how unconscious biases distort spiritual perception
Understanding how subtle discrimination undermines Christian community
Examining whether external actions truly reflect internal faith

socratic_nudge.question: If your faith were put on trial, would your actions be sufficient evidence of genuine belief?

moral_slider.question: How do personal preferences influence spiritual discernment?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of spiritual equality evolved?
options: Seek transformative understanding beyond social markers

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Impartiality
thematic_summaries.summary: God demonstrates no favoritism based on social status, wealth, or external appearances. True Christian faith requires treating all people with equal dignity and respect, reflecting Christ's inclusive love.

thematic_summaries.title: Faith and Works Demonstration
thematic_summaries.summary: Authentic faith is not merely intellectual assent but is actively demonstrated through compassionate actions and practical love. Genuine spiritual transformation produces visible evidence of internal spiritual renewal.

reevaluation.question: How does Biblical faith challenge cultural perspectives on social hierarchy and personal worth?
options: Spiritual value is independent of human classifications
Christian community should reflect radical inclusivity
